def: s.m. Mark of a paw (as a tiger's); impression or mark made with the palm of the hand on the wall of a house (as at a wedding, by the members of the household; or to mark out a house as one to be broken into and robbed; or to distinguish a house as that of a murderer, in which case the wall is smeared with blood); mark, impression, stamp (of a large and rude kind); gleanings (of corn, &c., left on the field after harvest).
more: n. m. 1. An impression of the palm of the hand on walls, made especially at a marriage by the members of the household. 2. Marks made on the unthreshed corn, left on the floor, in order to guard it against theft. thāp mārnā, yā lagānā , v. n. To clap (rice, dung cakes, etc.) against the wall. lahū ke thāpe , Blood stains smeared on the house of a murderer, or on a house on which a dacoity is to be committed.
s. m. A mark of the paw.

def: n. m. 1. A frame (esp. for thatching); frame-work; a skelton. 2. Fashion; mode; style; cut. Achchhā ṭhāṭ banāyā hai . 3. State; splendour; magnificence. 4. Establishment; equipage; furniture. 5. Scheme; plan; design; measure; policy. 1. Is arz o samā ke arse meṅ yeh jitnā khachcham khachchā hai, Ye ṭhāṭ tujhī ne bāṅdhā hai, aur raṅg tujhī ne rachchā hai! Nazīr. This earth and sky and space, with all its teeming struggling life! This grand design by Thee contrived, with divers colors rife! 2. Bahut uske bhārī thā sab ṭhāṭ bāṭ, Kare thā baṛī dhūm se rāj pāṭ . The plain truth; neither more nor less; just the thing; the very thing; to a hair; to an inch; to a T; to a tittle; to the letter; to a nicety; of a piece; true to nature; to the life; the very image; exactly so; as like as two peas; in all respects; in every respect; en regle; de jure; to the purpose; to the point; in point; apropos; to fit like a glove; right as a trivet; up to the mark.

s.f. = ṭheṅṭhī, q.v. s.f. A plug, cork, bung ( = ṭhepī; ṭhekī); a lurap or ball of ear-wax; a dhotī or cloth reaching as far as the knee.
s.m. Bed (of a garden); border.
s.m. = ṭheʼonā, q.v.
more: s. m. The knee; the pastern (of a horse).

def: s.f. Stopple, stopper, plug, cork, bung
more: s. f. A cork, a plug, a stopple. ٿهيپ منـﮩـ مين دينا ṭhepī muṅh meṅ denā, To be silent.
subs: adj. Stopped with a plug, having a stopper or plug or cork, corked
(kisī-ke), To stop the mouth (of anyone), to silence, to gag; to stop (one's own, -apne) mouth, to be silent.

def: Tir. n. f. A cork; plug; stopple; stopper. ṭhepī muṅh meṅ denā , v. a. To close one's mouth; to gag; silence. ṭhepī-dār , adj. Corked; stopped with a plug, etc.
n. f. 1. A knock; blow. 2. See ṭhokar , 1. ṭhes lagnā , v. n. To be knocked against; to trip against a stone, etc. Ṭhes lāge, budh bāṛhe . Prov. Get knocks, get sense.

s.f. Knock, blow; thrust, push, shove; striking the foot (against an obstacle), tripping (against); — ṭhes lagnā, v.n. To be knocked (against), to receive a knock, &c.; to trip (against = ṭhokar lagnā).
more: s. f. A knock, a blow, tripping against a stone, &c. a shove, a push, a thrust.

v.t. To thrust, to pierce; to knock, push, shove; to strike against; to thrust or ram in, to cram, stuff; to eat voraciously ( = ṭhosnā).
more: v. a. 1. To pierce. 2. To knock against. 3. To stuff, to cram, to ram in. 4. To eat ravenously.

def: s.m. Jeer, taunt, sneer, indirect reflection, innuendo.
more: s. m. Sneer, oblique reflection or innuendo.

def: Bhoj. n. m. A boy ( laṛkā ); son.
adj. Pure, genuine; real, true, actual, very; good, sound; chaste, idiomatic (as language); — contentious.
adj. H. ṭhīk right. 1. Sterling; standard; good. 2. Pure; genuine; unalloyed; idiomatic. Ṭheṭh Hindī . 3. Real; true. Ṭheṭh gaṅvār .
s.m. Setting (of a stone); stone set in a ring.
more: s. m. A stone set in a ring.

n. m. H. ṭhā place. The place for a stone in a ring.
s.m. The knee; the pastern (of a horse).
feminine singular imperfect indicative of ہونا (honā)

s.m. A mud pillar, a boundary mark ( = ṭhiyā, q.v.). s.m. A mound, heap, or lump (of earth); a block or mass (of stone, wood, &c.); a mud pillar, a boundary mark (cf. thūʼā; ṭhīhā; ḍhūʼā; ḍhiyā); a support; a place to sit on, a seat.

(caus. of تهجنا) v. a. (dakh.) To congeal ( Harris ).

def: adj. Firm, strong, compact; stable; ascertained, established, well-founded, certain; precise, exact, true, just, correct, accurate, right; even, complete; fit, meet, proper; reasonable; appropriate, suitable, apposite; corresponding, answering; regular, according to rule; in due form; well-formed, well-proportioned; straight, in order, well-conducted; — adv. Exactly, precisely, just; accurately, correctly; duly, justly, agreeably to; truly, in truth; to the point or purpose; completely; clearly, &c.; just so!, precisely!, very true!, all very fine!; — s.m. Precise total, sum, addition; — fixed or permanent residence; whereabouts ( = ṭhikānā); certainty, certain knowledge, &c. (in all these senses it seems questionable whether ṭhīk, although it may be conveniently rendered in English as a subst. = ṭhikānā, is not properly an adj.: it is invariably constructed with a genitive; e.g. uskā kyā ṭhīk hai, 'What is there certain in respect of him?' or 'What certainty is there of him?'; ṭumhārā bhī kahīṅ ṭhīk hai, 'Have you a fixed abode anywhere?')
more: 1. adj. Exact, even, accurate, complete, just, fit, meet, proper, reasonable, true, regular, right; adv. Exactly, &c. 2. ( met. ) Parsimonious. 3. s. m. Sum, addition. ٿهيک ٿهاک ṭhīk ṭhāk, adj. Exact, fit, proper, accurate; properly, fitly, accurately, adequately. ٿهيک آنا ṭhīk ānā, v. n. To fit, correspond, suit, answer, apply. ٿهيک ٿهاک کرنا ṭhīk ṭhāk karnā, v. a. To put to rights, correct, adjust, fit. ٿهيک کرنا ṭhīk karnā, v. a. 1. To put to rights, to correct, to adjust, to adapt, fit. 2. To beat.
subs: v.n. To prove true, turn out right or correct; to come right; to be right, just, or adequate; to succeed; to answer, suit, fit, correspond, apply; — ṭhīk banānā (-ko), To make right or exact, &c.; to put (one) to rights, to bring (one) to his senses; to beat ( = ṭhīk karnā)

adj. & adv. Exact, accurate; fit, proper; — quite right, right and tight, all right; properly, fitly, accurately, adequately; in due order, duly adjusted, put to rights, put straight, &c.; — s.m. Exactitude, &c.; — ṭhīk-ṭhāk karnā (-ko), To put to rights, to correct, adjust, fit, &c. ( = ṭhīk karnā)
adj. (emphat. of ṭhīk), The exact, very exact, &c. (see ṭhīk); approximate; tolerable, passable
ṭhīkam-ṭhīk, ṭhīkoṅ-ṭhīk, adv. Exactly, precisely, just, &c. = ṭhīk, q.v.

(-ko), To render exact, right, true, or fit, &c.; to correct, amend, rectify, put to rights, adjust, adapt; to settle, set in order, arrange; to complete, make up; to regulate; to ascertain, establish, verify; to make certain; to determine; to put (one) to rights, bring (one) to his senses, to beat, chastise
ex: اَپْنی غَلْطِیاں جَھٹ سے ٹِھیک کَرْنا چَاہِئے۔
apnī ġaltiyā̃ jhaṭ se ṭhīk karnā cāhie.
You should quickly correct your mistakes.

def: To ascertain.

adv. 1. All square; all right. 2. Repaired; done up; set up. ṭhīk miltī . Precisely applicable. ṭhīk jānnā, yā mānnā , v. a. To receive; allow; admit the accuracy of; approve. ṭhīk samajh . Sound mind; sound judgment; compos mentis; mens sana . ṭhīk samaë, yā waqt , Juncture; point of time; the nick of time; crisis. ṭhīk karnā , v. a. 1. To render true; to fit; adapt; adjust; square; reconcile. 2. To set; regulate; settle; arrange. 3. To rectify; put to rights; correct; alter. Meṅhdī maiṅ jab-tak lagāūṅ tab tak, aë manhyārī, tū Ṭhīk-kar joṛā, qarīne se lagā, rī chūṛyāṅ . Rangīn. While I put on the henna, bangle-maker, would just you Arrange the set of bangles in their place and fix them true. 4. To cure; remedy; reform; mend; amend. 5. To complete; finish; perfect; make up. 6. To make certain; ascertain; determine, get, or arrive, at the truth. 7. To make good; establish; substantiate; confirm; verify; prove. ṭhīk banānā, yā karnā v. a. To chastise; box one's ears; lick into shape; give a dressing; bring to one's senses or bearings. ghaṛī kī chāl ṭhīk karnā , v. a. To regulate a time-piece. ṭhīk na kahnā , v. n. To give crooked answers; to shuffle; fence; prevaricate; equivocate; to play fast and loose.

s.m. Contract; work done by contract or by the job, piece-work; a task, job; hire, fare; license; lease, farm; mortgage; rest (after fatigue); accompanying a singer (on the ḍhol or other musical instrument); andante (in music)
more: s. m. 1. Hire, fare, fixed price. 2. Work done by contract, a task.
subs: (-kā), To play an accompaniment, to accompany a singer (on the ḍhol, &c.)
s.f. A lease, a farm; a farm held on lease
s.m. A deed of lease, a document conveying a lease or farm
s.m. A lease or farm of which the rent is paid in advance
s.m. Contractor; lease-holder, farmer, lessee; one who farms a license (for the sale of spirituous liquors or the like)
(-ko), To give a contract (to), to lease, to let in farm; — ṭhekā dene-wālā, s.m. Lessor, grantor
s.f. The period for which a contract, &c., is granted; the term for which a lease has to run
(-kā), To take on contract, to contract (for); to lease, farm; to take out a license; to monopolize, engross
s.m. A lease for the term of one's life.

def: adv. Exactly, truly, rightly.
s. m. Abuse.
s. m. (dakh.) See ٿهکانا ṭhikānā.

ety: S. स्थिर+कृत+स्थान+कः; or ṭika, rt. of ṭiknā+S. स्थान+कः
def: s.m. Fixed or permanent place or abode; proper place; precise or exact spot, whereabouts; place, spot, site; dwelling.place, abode, residence, home, quarters; special field or scene of a labour, &c.), beat (as of a barber, or sweeper, &c.); fixing, fixity, certainty; certain knowledge; consistency, harmony, appositeness (of speech, conduct, &c.); origin, source; ground, basis, foundation (of a thing); goal, destination, end, limit, boundary
more: s. m. 1. Residence, place, station, site, abode, dwelling. 2. Boundary, limit, fixing. ٿهکانا ڐهونڐهنا ṭhikānā ḍhūndhnā, To seek a residence or employment. ٿهکانا کرنا ṭhikānā karnā, To account for, to search or trace an affair to its commencement. ٿهکانا لگانا ṭhikānā lagānā, 1. To establish, to settle, to prove. 2. To find out oneʼs residence. ٿهکاني لگانا ṭhikāne lagānā, 1. To put to death, to kill, to waylay, to make away with, despatch, to assassinate. 2. To establish, settle, arrange. ٿهکاني لگنا ṭhikāne lagnā, To be killed, to die, to be put an end to, to be terminated.
v. a. (dakh.) To cheat, to deceive, to impose on, defraud, beguile, bamboozle, delude, gull: see ٿهگنا ṭhagnā.
subs: To distribute money among servants (on the decease of a member of a family)
(-ke wāst̤e, &c.), To seek a residence or employment (for)
v.n. To remain stationary, fixed, or stable; to abide in one course, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Congruous, consistent, harmonious, apposite, to the point; sensible

(-kā), To put in (its) proper place; to find a place (for), to make room (for); to arrange; to take note (of), to learn (about), to account (for), to search or trace (an affair) to its source or commencement; to find a husband or wife (for)
(-kā), To find out, discover, trace (one's residence, &c.); to trace to the source, to establish, settle, prove
(-ko), To dispose of; to establish, settle, arrange; to make away with, to spend, consume, run through; to send to (his or her) long home, to despatch, put to death, kill, assassinate
v.n. To be still, be at rest, have a home, &c.; to be disposed of, be despatched, be put to death; to be put an end to, be terminated

adj. & adv. Out of place, misplaced; irregular; incoherent, disconnected; inconsistent; indeterminate, unsettled, indefinite; immoderate, disproportioned; uncertain; groundless, unreasonable; — irregularly, incoherently, &c.; indiscriminately, at random.
wety: Related to ٹھیک (ṭhīk). Cognate with Punjabi ਟਿਕਾਣਾ (ṭikāṇā) / ٹِکانا (ṭikānā), Gujarati ઠેકાણું (ṭhekāṇũ), Marathi ठिकाण (ṭhikāṇ), Bengali ঠিকানা (ṭhikana).

def: n. m. S. स्था to stand. 1. Place; site. See استهان (1, 2.) 2. Abode; residence. Tumhārā bhī kahīṅ ṭhikānā hai? 3. A community, or a collection of houses in which a barber, sweeper, etc. have a hereditary monopoly to pursue their profession. Ek ke ṭhikāne meṅ dūsrā halālkhor nahīṅ kamā saktā . 4. A direction or address ( patā , 2.). Sakhī vāhī des mohe jānā, Jā ko nāṅoṅ hai na ṭhikānā . Bhajan. Hymn. Friend, I for that strange land am bound, Whose name or sign-post none hath found. 5. Relations; relative; friends. 6. Centre; nucleus; point of attraction. Jahāṅ khānā, vahāṅ sab kā ṭhikānā . Prov. Where the dish goes round all wanderers (or addresses wanted) will be found. 7. End; limit. 8. Reliance; faith; trust; dependance. 1. Terī bāt kā kuchh ṭhikānā nahīṅ? 2. Divānah hai, valekin bāt kahtā hai ṭhikāne kī . Prov. Mad as he is, his words are full of meaning. ('There is method in his madness'). ṭhikāne chukānā , v. a. To distribute money among the servants on the decease of an aged member of the family. ṭhikānā ḍhūṅḍnā , v. a. To look out for a residence or employment. ṭhikānā k . v. a. 1. To arrange; manage. 2. To make room for. 3. Wom. To find a husband or wife for one. ṭhikānā lagānā , v. a. To find; find out, (one's residence, etc.); trace; detect. ṭhikāne lagānā , v. a. 1. To spend; consume; go through; finish. 2. To put to death; to kill. be-ṭhikāne , adj. 1. Misplaced; out of place; irregular. 2. Incoherent; unconnected; not related. 3. Indeterminate; unsettled; indefinite; vague. 4. Immoderate; disproportioned ( be- had ). 5. Precarious; uncertain. 6. Groundless; unreasonable; conjectural. be-ṭhikāne , adv. Indiscriminately; at random.
s.f. = ṭikānī, q.v. s.f. A prop for the support of the shafts or pole of a carriage (cf. tek).
n. f. A prop for the support of the shafts or pole of a carriage.
s.m. A system of ćaukī-dārī prevailing in certain parts of India (as in Rohtak Borī), the duty of keeping the village watch (taken by the villagers in turn; = thīkar).
n. The system of chaukī-dārī prevailing in Rohtak Berī.
s.m. The duty of keeping the village watch (taken in turn by the villagers).
s. (dakh.) Patch, botch ( Harris ): see تهگلا thiglā.
s.m. = ṭhīkrā, q.v. s.m. The duty of keeping the village watch (taken in turn by the villagers).
more: s. m. 1. Fragment of an earthen vessel, a shard, potsherd. 2. A vessel for carrying fire, such as faḳīrs use.

def: n. m. H. ṭukṛā a piece. 1. Potsherd; shard; a broken piece of any earthen-ware. 1. Hāth meṅ ṭhīkrā hogā, aur bhīk māṅgtā phiregā . A curse. A potsherd in your hand, at every door a beggar stand! ☞ Gālib, the witty poet of Delhi, relates that one day, as his servant was murmuring over the chilam which he filled with live coals from the ṭhīkrā , (fire place, also potsherd) for his master's hubble-bubble, he enquired what he had been saying to the ṭhīkrā potsherd. The servant replied that he had been saying 'how he had had no pay for eight months!' And what did the potsherd say?' He said 'Dont mind, I am with you' (' Ghabrā nahīṅ, maiṅ tere sāth hūṅ .') 2. Hāth meṅ ṭhīkrā, aur ṭhikre meṅ sattar chhed! Wom! May you beg from door to door, with a broken earthen pot full of holes (through which the alms you get may fall out)! 2. A broken vessel or ornament; a dilapidated house. 3. ( Contemp .) A vessel; a house. 4. Houses and lands. 5. An earthen pot for holding fire, filth, etc. ṭhīkrā phoṛnā , v. a. To slander one. buzurgoṅ kā ṭhīkrā , n. m. Houses or lands descended from one's ancestors by hereditary right. khāne, kamāne, yā rozī kā ṭhīkrā , n. m. 1. The tools or instruments by which one lives. 2. A girl brought up by the mother bawd.
s.m. The duty of keeping the village watch (taken in turn by the villagers).

s.m. A broken piece of earthenware, a large potsherd, a shard; a vessel for holding fire (such as faqīrs use, — usually the lower half of an earthen pitcher); — an earthen vessel for holding filth, &c.; a broken vessel or ornament; a dilapidated house; houses and lands
more: s. m. A broken piece of earthen ware.
subs: (-kā), (fig.) To slander, to defame; — rozī-kā ṭhīkrā, kamāne-kā ṭhīkrā, s.m. The tools, instruments, or means by which one lives.

def: s.f. = ṭhīkrī, q.v.; — also = ṭikrī, q.v.
more: s. f. Potsherd, shard: see ٿهيکري ṭhīkrī. ٿهکري مار ṭhikrī-mār (dakh.) A game amongst children, throwing a fragment of an earthen vessel so as to glide along the surface of the water ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: n. f. 1. Dim. of ṭhīkrā , q. v. 2. The upper or moveable jaw of the hammer of a musket. 3. Mons veneris .
s. (dakh.) Potsherd: see ٿهيکري ṭhīkrī.
s.f. A broken piece of earthen ware, potsherd; an earthen pan (for baking bread); the upper or movable jaw of the cock of a musket; mons veneris
more: s. f. 1. A broken piece of earthen ware, a potsherd. 2. Mons veneris. ٿهيکري چنا ṭhīkrī chunnā, To be mad.

s. f. (dakh.) Two bits of stick or fragments of earthenware used as castanets: or, two pieces of wood, one held between the fore and middle finger, the other between the middle and ring finger of the left hand; while, with the thumb and middle finger of the right hand, the ends of the sticks on the outside of the hand are made to strike against each other, producing the same sound as castanets ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s. m. Exactitude.
s. f. (dakh.) Purse, bag: see تهيلي thailī.
s.f. An earthen waterpot or pitcher
subs: s.m. lit. 'Filling a pitcher'; the ceremony of offering a libation of water to the manes of the deceased.

def: n. f. H. thal place. An earthen pitcher. Tāo ke din bā, ek ṭhilyā pānī meṅ kā hokhī? Bhoj. The day is warm, one pitcher of water is not enough. ṭhilyā bharnā , Hin. n. m. lit . To fill a pitcher with water. The rite of pouring a libation of water to the manes of the dead.
s.f. Sob; whimper, fretful cry (of a child).
v.n. To sob; to cry, whimper, fret (as a child); to whine, complain, murmur.
more: v. n. To sob. ¶ ṭhanaknā, v. n. 1. To throb, to shoot (as, the pain of headach). 2. To jingle, tinkle, clink.

def: s.f. (local), A spark ( = ćingī, ćingārī).
adj. (f. -ī), Of small stature, short, dwarfish, diminutive (syn. nāṭā).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀞𑀺𑀁𑀕 (*ṭhiṃga). Compare Punjabi ਠਿਗਣਾ (ṭhigṇā) / ٹھِگݨا (ṭhigṇā), Gujarati ઠીંગણું (ṭhī̃gṇũ), Marathi ठेंगणे (ṭheṅgṇe).

def: adj. Of small stature; middle sized.
s.f. Lowness of stature, dwarfishness, &c.
s.f. A vessel for holding or carrying fire (such as faqīrs use, — usually the lower half of an earthen pitcher, syn. ṭhikrā); a brasier, chafing-dish.
more: s. f. A kind of lamp.

def: adj. Fixed, firm, stable, permanent, durable; settled; tranquil, calm, smooth, even
more: adj. S. स्थिर See اتَل (1—4, 6, 7.) Sadā na joban thir rahe, sadā na sāvan hoë, Sadā na phūle ketkī, sadā na bar meṅ joë . Dohā. Not ever will your beauty last, not ever August rains will fall! Not always blooms the Kelkī flower, nor mistress loved your arms enthrall!
subs: v.n. To continue fixed or firm; to be durable, to last, &c.

def: adj. = thir, q.v. (vulg. or poetical). adj. Fixed, firm, stable, permanent, durable; settled; tranquil, calm, smooth, even
subs: v.n. To continue fixed or firm; to be durable, to last, &c.

def: s.f. Extreme cold; frost (cf. ṭhār; ṭhiṭhar).
adj. Difficult, hard, untoward, trying
subs: s.m. A hard or trying time.

def: v.n. To be set, be settled, be well postured (in dancing, &c.); to move or dance in a stately manner, or with expressive action and gesture; to strut, stalk; to mince, dance.
more: v. n. To mince; dance; shake; flourish; quiver; dance with expressive action and gesture. Wom. Zanān mantriyoṅ kī tarah thiraktā phirtā hai .
v. n. To set oneʼs self off well in dancing, to dance with expressive action and gesture.
v. n. (dakh) To hover (as a bird: Harris ).

def: v.n. = thiraknā, q.v. v.n. To be set, be settled, be well postured (in dancing, &c.); to move or dance in a stately manner, or with expressive action and gesture; to strut, stalk; to mince, dance.

def: v.n. To settle (as liquor); to subside (as a swelling, &c.); to become calm, or tranquil, &c.

more: (caus. of ٿهرنا) v. a. To cause to congeal. ¶ ṭharānā (caus. of ٿهرنا) v. a. To fix, settle; see ٿهـﮩرانا.

def: s.f. Firmness, stability, motionlessness, stillness, rest; steadfastness; settlement; calm, quiet, tranquillity, serenity.
more: n. f. 1. Rest; stillness. 2. Quiet; tranquility; calm; serenity.
s. f. Rest, settlement, tranquillity, steadfastness.

def: v. n. To cause to dance.
v. n. To be chilled; to freeze.
v.n. To freeze; to be chilled.
more: v. n. To freeze, to be chilled. ¶ ṭharnā, v. n. To be fixed or settled, to stop, to stand: see ٿهـﮩرنا ṭhaharnā.

def: v.n. = ṭhiṭhaknā, q.v. v.n. To stand still, to stagnate; to stop short or suddenly, to stand amazed or staggered; to draw back in surprise, to shrink, to hesitate, to boggle
subs: ṭhiṭhak-rahnā or rah-jānā, v.n. To stop short, &c. = ṭhiṭhaknā.

r: ṭhiṭak'nā, ṭhīṭhaknā, ṭhiṭak rahnā, yā jānā
def: v. n. S. तिष्ठ to stop. 1. To stop suddenly; draw back in surprise; stand amazed. 2. To stop short; hesitate; delay; boggle. ṭhiṭak ṭhiṭak-ke , adv. Hesitatingly; slowly ( āhistā ).

r: ṭhiṭar, ṭhiṭrā, ṭhiṭarnā, ṭhiṭhar, ṭhiṭhrā, ṭhiṭharnā
def: s.f. = H ٿهٿرا ठिटरा ṭhiṭrā, adj. = H ٿهٿرنا ठिटरना ṭhiṭarnā, v.n. see ṭhiṭhar; ṭhiṭhrā; and ṭhiṭharnā.

r: ṭhiṭarnā, ṭhiṭar, ṭhiṭrā, ṭhiṭhar, ṭhiṭhrā, ṭhiṭharnā
def: v.n. = H ٿهٿر ठिटर ṭhiṭar, s.f. = H ٿهٿرا ठिटरा ṭhiṭrā, adj. see ṭhiṭhar; ṭhiṭhrā; and ṭhiṭharnā.
v. n. 1. To be numbed, benumbed, chilled; to shiver. 2. To be stopped (growth); nipped in the bud. 1. Jāṛā ke māre hāth, goṛ ṭhiṭhural jā le . Mag. 2. Jāṛe meṅ paude ṭhiṭar jāte haiṅ .
s.f. The state of standing still, stagnation; the state of stopping suddenly, or of standing amazed, &c.; — (conj. part. of next), adv. Reluctantly, hesitatingly, slowly ( = ṭhiṭhak-ke).
more: (from ٿهٿهکنا) s. f. The state of standing amazed. ٿهٿهک رهنا يا جانا ṭhiṭhak-rahnā or -jānā, v. n. To stop short.

def: adv. In a state of amazement, staggered; reluctantly, shrinkingly, hesitatingly, slowly.

ety: ṭhiṭhak˚ = Prk. ठडुक्के(इ), fr. S. स्तब्ध+कृ
def: v.n. To stand still, to stagnate; to stop short or suddenly, to stand amazed or staggered; to draw back in surprise, to shrink, to hesitate, to boggle
more: v. n. To stop, to stagnate, to stand amazed, to draw back in surprise, to shrink, to boggle.
subs: ṭhiṭhak-rahnā or rah-jānā, v.n. To stop short, &c. = ṭhiṭhaknā.

def: s.f. Numbedness, numbness, torpor, chilliness, cold; frost.

ety: S. स्थिर redupl., or pres. part. तिष्ठत्, rt. स्था
def: adj. Benumbed, numb, torpid, chilled; impotent
v.n. To be chilled (with cold); to be frozen; to be benumbed or numbed; to be stopped (growth), to be nipped (with cold)
more: v. n. To be numbed or benummed, to be chill.

def: s.f. = ṭheṅ-ṭheṅ.
v.t. To chill, to bennmb, to numb.
more: &c. (caus. of ٿهٿهرنا) v. a. To chill, benum, numb.

def: s.f. Numbness, &c. = ṭhiṭhar, q.v. s.f. Numbedness, numbness, torpor, chilliness, cold; frost.
more: (from ٿهٿهرنا) s. f. Numbedness, torpor, torpidness.

def: s.f. Permanence, continuance, stability; staying; stay; steadiness, consistency; keeping, bringing up, fostering.
adj. 1. Benumbed; numb; torpid. 2. Impotent.

r: ṭhiṭrā, ṭhiṭar, ṭhiṭarnā, ṭhiṭhar, ṭhiṭhrā, ṭhiṭharnā
def: adj. = H ٿهٿر ठिटर ṭhiṭar, s.f. = H ٿهٿرنا ठिटरना ṭhiṭarnā, v.n. see ṭhiṭhar; ṭhiṭhrā; and ṭhiṭharnā.
v. a. To chill; benumb; numb.
s.m. = ṭheʼonā, q.v.
s.m. A mound, heap, or lump (of earth); a block or mass (of stone, wood, &c.); a mud pillar, a boundary mark (cf. thūʼā; ṭhīhā; ḍhūʼā; ḍhiyā); a support; a place to sit on, a seat.
n. m. 1. A boundarymark. 2. A seat; the place where one sits.
s.m.(?) Number (local, and used in comp. with numerals, e.g. do ṭho, 'two'; syn. ʻadad).
(In comp). Number. Do ṭho . Two.

ety: थोथु or S. प्रोथ inverted+ṛ & ṛā = Prk. अडो or अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. lit. 'Nose or snout of an animal'; (in contempt) mouth, muzzle, (slang) mug (syn. thoth).
Hin. n. f. See پيندَّي

ety: थोथु or S. प्रोथ inverted+ṛ & ṛā = Prk. अडो or अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. lit. 'Nose or snout of an animal'; (in contempt) mouth, muzzle, (slang) mug (syn. thoth).
n. m. The mouth ( contempt .); muzzle ( thothnī ).

s.m. Multitude, mass, quantity; clump, heap; body, band, company, party, community; amount, total sum; ready money; share, portion, allotment, lot; holding, tenure, local division (of an estate); a subdivision in coparcenary (bhaʼiyā ćārī) estate; a spot where three or more boundary lines meet, junction of boundaries
more: n. m. S. स्तोम multitude. 1. A company; class; party; community; band ( giroh ). 2. A total amount; a sum. 3. An allotted portion; a share; lot; portion. 4. A tenure; holding; a local division of an estate. 5. A sub-division in a bhaīyā chārī or coparcenary estate. 6. A point where three or more boundary lines meet.
s. m. 1. Amount, heap, ready money. 2. Share, portion. تهوک دار thok-dār, s. m. A wholesale dealer.
subs: (-kā), To form into parties or companies, &c.; to form into lots; to fix the limits of estates (by a native survey)
s.f. Fixing the limits of estates (by a native survey, or preparatory to a regular scientific survey), laying down and marking off boundaries (of an estate, &c.)
s.m. The head of a company, or community; the holder of a thok; the holder of a principa share (in a village, who is responsible for the payment of the revenue); — a wholesale dealer; a copyholder.

def: wholesaler
s.m. (dialec.), Blow, stroke, &c. ( = ṭahokā; ṭhokar).
s.m. (dialec.), Blow, stroke, &c. ( = ṭahokā; ṭhokar).
s.m. The act of making firm, the act of driving in (a peg, &c.), or of beating, or striking.

ety: rt. of ṭhoknā+ar = Prk. अडी = S. र+ई
def: s.f. A cause of stumbling (as projecting stones, boulders, &c. on a road), obstacle, stumbling-block; tripping or striking the foot against anything; trip, stumble, false step; kick, thump, blow, stroke; shock; loss (in trade, &c.); beating time with the foot; a cow-catcher (syn. ćhāj)
more: s. f. 1. Tripping or striking the foot against any thing, a beat of the foot, a kick, a stumble. 2. A stumblingblock. ٿهوکر کهانا ṭhokar khānā, 1. To trip, to stumble. 2. To meet with a loss or misfortune. ٿهوکرلگنا ṭhokar lagnā, To strike against the foot (a stumblingblock or obstacle).
subs: v.t. To kick away, to spurn
v.n. To trip, stumble; to meet with a loss, to suffer misfortune
v.n. To trip, &c. ( = ṭhokar khānā); to be kicked and knocked about, to be ill-used (by, -kī)
(-kī), To come into collision (with), to knock (against, an obstacle), to trip, stumble
To kick (against, -par), knock or strike (against); to strike, kick, spurn (with, -ko).

def: n. f. 1. Tripping or striking the foot accidentally against anything; a false step; a trip; stumble. 1. Ṭhokar khāve, budh pāve . Prov. From knocks received without good sense is driven within. 2. Jo sir uṭhā-ke chalega, so ṭhokar khāëgā . Prov. Who walk with head uplifted high will trip and prostrate lie. 2. A kick. 3. Projecting stones, etc. on the road; a bad road. Ṭhokar hai! (Kahārs' idiom). 4. Shock; blow; loss. Sau rupaë kī ṭhokar lagī . 5. A cow-catcher ( chhāj ). 6. Beating time with the foot. Banā-ke saj dhaj, phirā-ke dāman, lagā-ke ṭhokar, hilā-ke bālā . Nazīr. Her person all adorned, her skirt coquettish jerked, a mincing gait assumed, her earrings frequent shook. 7. A pair of shoes (Gamblers' idiom). ṭhokar par mārnā , v. n. To kick away; to spurn. ṭhokar khānā , v. n. 1. To stumble. 2. To suffer; sustain a loss. Ādmī ṭhokar khā-kar saṅbhaltā hai . Prov. Stumble and get sense. ṭhokaren khānā , v. n. 1. ( ṭhokar lagnā ). To trip; stumble. Ṭhokar lagī pahāṛ kī, aur toṛeṅ ghar kī sil . Prov. He hurts his foot against the rocks, the grinding stone to pieces knocks. (He vents his anger on his wife). 2. To be kicked, or made a foot ball of. Bin maiyā kā bachchā ṭhokreṅ khātā phirtā hai . Prov. Saying. The motherless child gets knocks and blows where'er he goes. 3. To be sat upon; to have to bear any harshness or insult. Kyoṅ apne parāyoṅ kī ṭhokreṅ khāūṅ? Wom. ṭhokar mārnā , v. n. 1. To strike; knock on the head; kick. 2. To kick away; turn up one's nose at.
n. f. Laying down and marking off the boundaries; fixing the limits of estates by a native survey, or preparatory to a professional survey. thok bāṅdhnā , v. a. To form into parties.
alternative form of تھوک دان (thok dān)
n. m. 1. The holder of a thok . 2. The head of a company or society. 3. A wholesale dealer; a copyholder.

ety: ṭhok˚ = Prk. टुक्ख(इ) = S. त्वक्ष(ति), rt. त्वक्ष्
def: v.t. To make firm, to drive in (a stake, peg, &c.); to hammer, strike, beat, thump, knock; to pat, tap, fillip; to thrust with the finger; to play, perform on (the kettle-drum); to put in the stocks; to enter (a complaint against), file (a suit or action)
more: v. a. To beat, to strike, to drive (as, a stake).
subs: adv. After striking and sounding, after close examination or scrutiny; openly, publicly
(-meṅ), To put a spoke (in one's wheel)
(-kā), To make a lock secure, to lock or shut up.

def: v. a. 1. To drive in (a stake, etc). Kīl ṭhoknā . 2. To bang; thwack; lay on; whallop. 3. To punish; serve out; castigate ( pīṭnā 2, 3). Masjid meṅ vāëzoṅ ke taīṅ lag gaī hai baṛ, Zāhik ne ṭhokā Shai/?/kh ko pagṛī utār-kar . Saudā. In mosque the priest his voice still sounds his doctrines to propound, The turban of the priest knocked off, his head the wag did sound. 4. To enter a suit or complaint against. 5. To put in the stocks. 6. To pat on the back. Kamar ṭhoṅknā. ṭhok bajā-ke , adv. 1. After close examination. 2. Openly; publicly. ṭhok bajā-ke lenā, ṭhok bajā-ke dekhnā , v. n. To sound (coin, crockery, etc.). 1. Dekhī ṭhok bajā-ke, dunyā tālib zar kī . Prov. The world on money-getting keen, its soundness oft assayed has been. 2. Damṛī kī hāṅḍī bhī ṭhok bajā-ke lete haiṅ . Prov. A farthing's earthen pot you buy, its soundness oft you sound and try. baṅgā ṭhoknā , v. a. 1. To put a spoke in one's wheel. 2. ( Slang ). To harrass; annoy. tālā ṭhoknā , v. a. To lock, shut, or seal up. arzī ṭhoknā , v. n. To file a suit or action.

ety: rt. of ṭhoknā+Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A fillip; — adj. (f. -ī), Hard (as bad bread).
more: 1. s. m. A fillip. 2. adj. Hard (as, bad bread).

ety: ṭhoka, fr. ṭhoknā+S. कः, with euphonic w
def: s.m. (prov.) lit. 'That which is patted'; a cake, a pancake
s.m. = thoṛ, q.v. s.f. A net thrown over a woman's pālkī.
more: E. n. m. The e/?/r of rice not yet out of its spathe.

def: s.m. A cup for the food and drink of a bird in a cage; — the knuckles
more: s. m. 1. A cup for the food and drink of a bird in a cage. 2. The knuckles. ٿهولامارنا ṭholā mārnā, To strike with the knuckles.

def: s.f. = ṭhoṛhī or ṭhoṛī, q.v. s f. The chin ( = ṭhuḍḍhī or ṭhuḍḍī, q.v.)
subs: ṭhoṛī-meṅ hāth denā (-kī) = ṭhuḍḍī pakaṛnā, q.v.
